# MACRO REPORT — 2026-04-19
**Delivered:** 11:21 UTC | **Generated by:** Claude claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

The dominant macro narrative entering the week of April 19 is the tentative de-escalation of the Middle East conflict, centred on Iran's reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and progress toward a US-Iran peace deal — a development that triggered a sharp 9% sell-off in oil prices, a record-breaking equity rally, and a partial reset of Fed rate-cut expectations [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-17-2026]. The single most important variable for today's session is the outcome of this weekend's second round of US-Iran talks and whether the Hormuz ceasefire holds, which will determine whether the geopolitical risk premium in energy continues to unwind or re-inflates. With the S&P 500 hitting all-time highs, VIX at 17.48, and a heavy earnings week ahead (94 S&P 500 companies including Tesla), risk appetite is elevated but fragile [Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/bill_stone/2026/04/19/tesla-leads-a-busy-earnings-week-with-94-sp-500-reports/]. The IMF has warned that the Middle East war "halted" global economic momentum, forecasting 2.3% US GDP growth for 2026 — and with CPI still running at 3.32% and the Fed on hold at 3.64%, the path for rate cuts remains narrow but is beginning to re-open [Source: https://www.newsweek.com/map-shows-countries-taking-biggest-hit-iran-war-11833834].

---

## 1. GLOBAL MACRO OVERVIEW

US equity markets closed at record highs last Friday in one of the fastest turnarounds in years. The S&P 500 rose +1.2% to 7,126.06 and the Nasdaq 100 gained +1.29% to 26,672.43, with Barclays noting the S&P 500's round trip from near-correction to all-time high took only 54 trading days — the fastest since 2020 [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/news/stock-market-notches-one-of-its-fastest-turnarounds-in-years-112336846.html]. The catalyst was Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi declaring the Strait of Hormuz "completely open" to commercial shipping, sending oil plummeting and risk assets surging. The Dow Jones surged +1.79% to 49,447.43 while the VIX fell -2.56% to 17.48, signalling a meaningful easing of near-term stress [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/news/stock-market-notches-one-of-its-fastest-turnarounds-in-years-112336846.html].

The macro backdrop remains complex. US CPI stands at 3.32% YoY and PPI at 1.78% MoM as of March 2026, still well above the Fed's 2% target [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/CPIAUCSL]. The Federal Funds Effective Rate sits at 3.64%, with the 10-year Treasury yield at 4.32% and the 2-year at 3.78%, leaving a 54bps positive slope to the curve [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DGS10]. Fed Governor Waller acknowledged that if the Middle East conflict resolves quickly, he would be "more inclined toward cuts to support the labour market later this year," while Deutsche Bank has taken the opposite view, expecting rates to be held throughout 2026 amid oil-driven inflation risks [Source: https://www.reuters.com/business/deutsche-bank-expects-fed-hold-rates-2026-2026-04-17/].

The IMF's Spring Meetings in Washington were dominated by the energy shock from the Middle East war. The IMF warned the conflict had "halted positive global momentum," pledging up to $150 billion combined with the World Bank for developing nations hit by energy price shocks [Source: https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/imf-world-bank-meetings-show-limits-mitigating-shocks-reliance-us-solutions-2026-04-19/]. The IMF forecasts US GDP growth of 2.3% for 2026, marginally below its January estimate. The ECB meanwhile faces a complex decision with approximately two weeks until its next meeting, navigating a "layer cake of shocks" with traders pricing ECB rates reaching at least 2.5% by year-end, representing 50+ basis points of hikes from current levels [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/16/ecb-interest-rates-hike-inflation-iran-washington.html].

The earnings season is ramping up significantly this week. With 10% of S&P 500 companies having reported, 88% have beaten consensus estimates, and the blended earnings growth rate stands at 13.1% YoY — marginally below the 13.2% expected at quarter-end [Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/bill_stone/2026/04/19/tesla-leads-a-busy-earnings-week-with-94-sp-500-reports/]. Tesla leads a busy week with 94 S&P 500 companies and seven Dow components scheduled to report. A cooler-than-expected PPI print combined with strong bank earnings from JPMorgan and Bank of America has helped equity sentiment remain resilient despite the geopolitical uncertainty [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/news/stock-market-notches-one-of-its-fastest-turnarounds-in-years-112336846.html].

---

## 2. CONFLICT / GEOPOLITICAL STATUS

**MIDDLE EAST WAR — IRAN / STRAIT OF HORMUZ**

- Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz "completely open" to commercial vessels for the duration of a ceasefire period, triggering a ~9% collapse in global oil prices on Friday. US-Iran peace talks entered a second round this weekend with markets closely watching for a durable resolution. [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-17-2026]
- Oil markets had been disrupted for approximately 45 days by the Strait of Hormuz blockade, with roughly 20% of global oil and LNG supplies at risk. Goldman Sachs noted demand losses in early 2026 were "larger than more dramatic oil price spikes in 2011 and 2022." [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-17/goldman-sees-softer-oil-demand-flags-two-sided-risks-2026-price]
- The IEA warned that oil and gas recovery infrastructure could take up to two years to normalise after war damage, even if peace is achieved. [Source: https://oilprice.com/Latest-Energy-News/World-News/IEA-Chief-Says-Oil-Gas-Recovery-Could-Take-Two-Years-After-War-Damage.html]
- US-Iran talks are expected to span past summer as oil flows remain disrupted via alternative routing. NATO allies declined to participate in a proposed Strait of Hormuz blockade, creating geopolitical fragmentation. [Source: https://oilprice.com/Energy/Oil-Prices/Oil-Slides-but-the-Real-Test-Comes-This-Weekend.html]
- Trump says Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a ceasefire, a key secondary de-escalation that supported the equity rally on Thursday/Friday. [Source: https://www.barrons.com/livecoverage/stock-market-news-today-041626]
- The IMF and World Bank Spring Meetings in Washington shifted from "gloom to tentative optimism" as Iran's Hormuz reopening emerged. The IMF warned the conflict has "halted" global economic momentum. [Source: https://www.reuters.com/world/asia-pacific/imf-world-bank-meetings-show-limits-mitigating-shocks-reliance-us-solutions-2026-04-19/]
- Commodity currencies (AUD, CAD, NZD, NOK) are seen as structural beneficiaries of the new geopolitical commodity order being reshaped by the war. [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-17/commodities-reshape-geopolitics-currency-pecking-order-gets-reset]

## 5. GLOBAL EQUITY INDICES

### US Equities

**S&P 500 — ~7,126.06** | Bias: Bullish
The S&P 500 closed at record highs on April 17, up +1.2%, completing what Barclays described as "one of the fastest turnarounds in years" — a round trip from near-correction (-9%) to all-time high in just 54 trading days [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/news/stock-market-notches-one-of-its-fastest-turnarounds-in-years-112336846.html]. The rally is being powered by semiconductors, internet/media, and hardware stocks as AI-themed buying returns. With 88% of early reporters beating estimates and blended earnings growth at 13.1% YoY, the fundamental backdrop supports further upside, though valuations at record highs leave the index vulnerable to any peace-deal disappointment [Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/bill_stone/2026/04/19/tesla-leads-a-busy-earnings-week-with-94-sp-500-reports/]. The Nasdaq hit its longest winning streak since 2009 at 12 consecutive sessions [Source: https://www.barrons.com/livecoverage/stock-market-news-today-041626].

**Nasdaq 100 — ~26,672.43** | Bias: Bullish
The Nasdaq 100 gained +1.29% last Friday with tech leading the broader market rally. The index is at record highs and benefiting disproportionately from AI-driven earnings momentum and the ebbing of Middle East inflation fears that had weighed on long-duration growth stocks. Tesla earnings this week will be the critical test for the Magnificent 7 narrative [Source: https://www.forbes.com/sites/bill_stone/2026/04/19/tesla-leads-a-busy-earnings-week-with-94-sp-500-reports/].

**Dow Jones — ~49,447.43** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
The Dow surged +1.79% to 49,447.43 with broad participation including smaller-cap rotation, as the Russell 2000 also hit a record high — its first since January 22. The Dow's strength reflects a broadening of the equity rally beyond mega-cap tech into industrials and financials [Source: https://www.barrons.com/livecoverage/stock-market-news-today-041626].

### Asia

**Nikkei 225 — ~58,475.90** | Bias: Neutral-Bear
The Nikkei fell -1.75% on April 17, underperforming its global peers as USD/JPY dynamics and energy cost concerns weighed on Japanese corporates. Japan's heavy energy import dependence makes the Nikkei particularly sensitive to oil price spikes, though Friday's oil decline should provide some relief in the next session. [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/]

**KOSPI — ~6,191.92** | Bias: Neutral
The KOSPI declined -0.55%, reflecting South Korea's vulnerability to energy import costs and semiconductor export uncertainty. The Bank of Korea faces similar dilemmas to other central banks in balancing growth support against energy-driven inflation. [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/]

**ASX 200 — ~8,946.90** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
The ASX 200 was nearly flat (-0.09%), holding near elevated levels as Australia's commodity export sector provides a structural buffer. With AUD leading G10 peers and commodity currencies broadly outperforming, the ASX's resource-heavy composition is well-positioned for a peace-deal scenario. [Source: https://finance.yahoo.com/]

### Europe

**DAX — ~24,702.24** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
The DAX surged +2.27% on April 17, the strongest performer among global indices in the bundle, driven by energy cost relief as Brent oil tumbled. Germany's industrial economy is acutely sensitive to energy prices, making the Hormuz reopening especially positive for DAX earnings expectations. The ECB's uncertain rate path (with 50+ bps of hikes possibly priced in for 2026) remains an overhang, but near-term risk appetite is clearly bullish [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/16/ecb-interest-rates-hike-inflation-iran-washington.html].

| Bear | 20% | Peace talks collapse; Hormuz re-blockaded; oil spikes above $100; Fed hawkish reaction forces risk-off; earnings disappoint | $105 | 6,600 | $65,000 |

**Key Upcoming Catalysts — Week of April 19-25, 2026:**

1. **US-Iran Peace Talks (Weekend/Monday)** — Second round of negotiations. Market binary: deal or breakdown will set the tone for oil, equities, and rate cut expectations for the entire week.
2. **Tesla Q1 Earnings (Week of April 21)** — Leads the busiest stretch of earnings season with 94 S&P 500 companies reporting; Tesla is the bellwether for AI/tech and EV demand narratives.
3. **US Flash PMI Data (April 22-23)** — Manufacturing and services PMI will reveal how badly the Middle East war has dented US business sentiment; key input for the Fed's assessment.
4. **ECB Rate Decision (~April 30, within two weeks)** — Markets pricing ECB at 2.5% by year-end (50+ bps of hikes); the April meeting setup could see hawkish guidance given sticky energy-driven inflation [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/16/ecb-interest-rates-hike-inflation-iran-washington.html].
5. **Lam Research & ServiceNow Earnings (Week of April 21)** — High historical beat rates (92% and above) and AI-leverage make these key reads on tech capex and software demand trends [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/17/these-stocks-with-earnings-next-week-historically-beat-expectations.html].
